response.justification: The candidate is a capable and versatile senior engineer with strong web, full-stack, and some systems-level experience, but he does not meet the core hard requirements for this role. There is no indication of native-level Japanese proficiency (languages listed are English, Romanian, and basic German), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and only brief professional C work (six months at National Instruments) versus the required strong C for UNIX. He also shows no background in embedded networking, LAN switches, or the listed security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or tamper detection.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/hardware-adjacent projects
Solid engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and low-latency asynchronous systems work in DLang
Comfortable with Linux, Docker, and building development environments and tooling from scratch
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed, which is a hard requirement (native-level)
No Linux kernel analysis/modification experience and only limited professional C programming
No exposure to the role's security and networking domain (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, SNMP/NETCONF, switch-chip SDKs)

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
Strong general engineering and Linux/systems fundamentals
